description:
You are an edge-computing engineer about to roll out code to several IoT gateways.  
Before deployment you want a **parallel, repeatable network-reachability test** for every
gateway listed in the inventory file that already exists on the system.

Your job:

1. Inventory file  
   • Location: /home/user/iot_targets.csv  
   • Format (comma separated, **no spaces**):  
     device_id,ip  
     Example lines already in the file:  
     device01,127.0.0.1  
     device02,10.255.255.1  

2. What must be done  
   a. Read the inventory file.  
   b. For every line (skip blank / comment lines) launch an **ICMP ping test in parallel**  
      (background jobs, xargs -P, GNU parallel, etc.—implementation is up to you, but they
      must run truly concurrently).  
      • Exactly 3 echo-requests (`-c 3`) per host.  
      • A per-host overall timeout of **7 seconds** so the workflow never hangs.  
   c. Determine status for each host:  
      – “OK”      → at least one response was received (non-zero packets received).  
      – “UNREACHABLE” → zero packets received *or* command times out.  
   d. Extract the **average round-trip-time** (RTT) from the ping output **only if** the
      status is OK.  
      – If status is UNREACHABLE write “NA” instead of a number.  
   e. Write one line per host to a new diagnostics log file whose name embeds a
      timestamp, and also generate/update a stable symlink.  

3. Required artefacts  
   • Directory: /home/user/logs   (create it if missing)  
   • Log file name pattern  
     /home/user/logs/network_diagnostics_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.log  
     – Use 24-hour time, zero-padded to two digits.  
   • Log file content (one host per line, **comma separated, no spaces**):  
     device_id,ip,icmp_status,avg_latency_ms  
     Example of a *valid* file with two entries (numbers will differ):  
     device01,127.0.0.1,OK,0.042  
     device02,10.255.255.1,UNREACHABLE,NA  
   • Symlink: /home/user/logs/latest_network_diagnostics.log → must point to the file
     created in the current run *and must be overwritten every time the task is re-run*.  

4. Parallelism & robustness requirements  
   • The ping tests have to be started so they overlap in time. It must be observable in
     `ps` or with a short sleep that multiple `ping` processes run simultaneously.  
   • Your script/commands must exit with code 0 even when some devices are
     UNREACHABLE.  
   • Under no circumstance should any single ping block the whole run for more than the
     7-second per-host timeout.  

5. Verification hint  
   After successful completion the new timestamped file *and* the
   latest_network_diagnostics.log symlink must exist, and the file must contain exactly
   as many data lines as there are valid, non-empty lines in /home/user/iot_targets.csv.
   The status/latency columns must follow the rules in step 3.

Create everything from the command line (no manual text editors).  
Feel free to display helpful console output, but **do not** deviate from the file names
and formats laid out above.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:

1. File /home/user/iot_targets.csv already exists with the following two lines and a trailing newline:
device01,127.0.0.1
device02,10.255.255.1

2. Directory /home/user/logs does NOT exist.

Expected state after correct task execution (the actual timestamp varies, here we
illustrate with 20250130_154210):

1. A new directory /home/user/logs is present.

2. A new regular file
   /home/user/logs/network_diagnostics_20250130_154210.log
   exists with mode 0644 owned by user:user.

   The file has exactly two lines (newline-terminated UTF-8 text):
   device01,127.0.0.1,OK,<FLOAT>
   device02,10.255.255.1,UNREACHABLE,NA

   where <FLOAT> is a positive decimal RTT value such as 0.041 or 0.17 matching regex
   ^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$ .  The second line must literally contain “UNREACHABLE,NA”.

3. The symlink /home/user/logs/latest_network_diagnostics.log exists and resolves to
   the exact file created in step 2.  Any previous symlink is overwritten.

4. There are no extra files in /home/user/logs other than:
   latest_network_diagnostics.log  (symlink)
   network_diagnostics_*.log       (exactly one new file)

5. The agent’s commands must have launched at least two concurrent `ping` processes for
   a non-trivial overlap period (≥1 second).  This will be verified by sampling the
   process table twice shortly after the agent starts the pings and confirming both
   destinations appear concurrently.

6. The agent’s top-level command sequence returns exit code 0.

A hidden validator will:
• readlink /home/user/logs/latest_network_diagnostics.log and compare path basename to
  pattern network_diagnostics_*.log
• check the content lines count (must equal 2) and exact CSV ordering
• confirm “OK” / “UNREACHABLE,NA” appear for the correct IPs
• validate the float field on the OK line using regex ^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$
• ensure the UNREACHABLE line has “NA” exactly
